title: Spot the Meaningful Change (Non-synonymous SNP)
learning_goal: Learn that a SNP can change a codon in a way that DOES change the resulting amino acid. This is called a non-synonymous (missense) mutation, and it can alter how the protein works.
rules: You'll work with whichever gene you chose in Level 1 - your choice carries over automatically.
Both the reference and your sample DNA sequences are shown in full right away - your job is to spot the ONE nucleotide that differs between them.
Click directly on the nucleotide in the sample row that you think differs from the reference.
Once you find it, the affected codon's amino acid is blanked out - use the codon table to work out what it should be and fill it in yourself.
This time, check whether that single nucleotide change actually changes the amino acid too.
materials_provided: The reference (known/healthy) nucleotide sequence and full amino acid sequence, shown on screen the whole time
Your sample's full nucleotide sequence, shown alongside the reference so you can click to locate the ONE nucleotide difference
An amino acid picker (all 20 amino acids plus the '*' STOP symbol) to fill in the blanked codon once you've found the SNP
instructions: 1. Compare the reference DNA row against your sample DNA row, letter by letter, and click the ONE nucleotide in the sample that differs (same codon position as Level 2, so you can directly compare outcomes).
2. The codon containing that nucleotide is revealed as a blank in your protein row. Look up the changed codon in the codon table and click the amino acid you think it now codes for.
3. Once correct, compare your completed protein row against the reference protein row. This time they are DIFFERENT at the SNP position.

4. The game confirms: 'Non-synonymous (missense) mutation - the protein has changed!' and explains how a changed amino acid could affect how the protein works, hinting at how such changes matter for real pathogens.

mutation_details.change: A -> G at the 1st position of codon 9
mutation_details.classification: Non-synonymous (missense) SNP - ATT (Isoleucine) becomes GTT (Valine), so the protein sequence changes at position 9.

mutation_details.change: G -> A at the 1st position of codon 9
mutation_details.classification: Non-synonymous (missense) SNP - GTT (Valine) becomes ATT (Isoleucine), so the protein sequence changes at position 9.

mutation_details.change: A -> C at the 1st position of codon 9
mutation_details.classification: Non-synonymous (missense) SNP - AAA (Lysine) becomes CAA (Glutamine), so the protein sequence changes at position 9.

compare_with_level_2: Same codon (#9) as level 2, but the changed nucleotide position within the codon is different (1st position here vs 3rd/wobble position in level 2). This lets players directly see why WHERE a SNP lands in a codon matters as much as whether it occurs at all.
